description: Cooked meals and hearty dishes common across most settings.
foods: Stew
A thick bubbling pot of whatever was on hand, the kind of meal that fixes most problems.
Soup
Thin and hot and honest, served in deep bowls that warm your hands before your stomach.
Roast
Slow-cooked until the meat falls apart at a look, filling the room with a smell that draws everyone in.
Meat Pie
A golden-topped pie with steam escaping from a slit in the crust, hiding something rich underneath.
Fried Fish
Crisp on the outside and flaky within, eaten with fingers and best near the water where it was caught.
Grilled Skewer
Chunks of meat and vegetables threaded on a stick and charred over open flame until the edges blacken.
Stuffed Pepper
A bright pepper hollowed out and packed with seasoned grain and herbs, roasted until it softens.
Bean Stew
Humble and thick, flavored with whatever herbs grew nearby, the meal of people who waste nothing.
Savory Pancake
A thin crispy cake folded around fillings, eaten standing up at market stalls.
Pot Roast
Cooked low and slow in a covered pot until the vegetables dissolve into the sauce.
